title: T-136 — Foundation AppShell browser error-discovery spec + parity report (Playwright)
prompt:
# T-136 — Foundation AppShell browser error-discovery spec

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Deliver the wave's final parity gate: a Playwright spec that visits every shell-relevant route in a real browser, fails closed on the listed categories of browser errors, and writes a machine-readable `parity_report.json` with `recommended_followups[]` that a future Opus run can convert into ACP tasks. This task DOES NOT create ACP tasks itself — it only writes the report and lets the operator decide.

## Source
Brief `/tmp/monopilot-shell-gap-brief-combined.md` §3 Luka H + §8 (F-AS-T07 contract) + final Spark-style audit summary ("Browser gate must fail on pageerror/console.error/requestfailed/404/500 and produce follow-up evidence"). The brief's §8 originally said `error_discovery_mode: true` / non-failing WARN; this wave OVERRIDES that — the spec MUST fail closed on the listed categories. The user-facing report still carries the `recommended_followups[]` array for human triage.

## Files (scope)
- `apps/web/e2e/shell-smoke.spec.ts` [create — Playwright spec; uses the project's auth-storage fixture from T-123]
- `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/.gitkeep` [create]
- `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/parity_report.schema.json` [create — JSON Schema describing the report shape so consumers (Opus, dashboards) can parse it deterministically]
- `apps/web/e2e/_helpers/shell-parity.ts` [create — helpers: `installConsoleSpy(page)`, `installNetworkSpy(page)`, `assertShellRegions(page, expected)`, `buildReportEntry(...)`, `writeReport(reportPath, entries)`]

Do NOT modify `apps/web/proxy.ts`, any AppShell product file, or any T-XXX.json outside this wave. Do NOT shell out to ACP or create tasks programmatically.

## Routes visited (per spec)
| URL | Auth state | expects_shell | expects_subnav | active_nav_item |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `/en/` | authed | true | none | `dashboard` |
| `/en/settings` | authed | true | `settings` | `settings` |
| `/en/settings/users` | authed | true | `settings` | `settings` (sidebar) + `users` (subnav) |
| `/en/settings/roles` | authed | true | `settings` | `settings` (sidebar) + `roles` (subnav) |
| `/en/login` | unauthed | false | none | n/a |
| `/en/dev/scanner` | authed (scanner harness — auth model TBD by scanner module) | false (ScannerFrame instead) | none | n/a |
| `/pl/login` | unauthed | false | none | n/a (locale mirror) |

## Failure conditions (the spec FAILS the run if ANY of these fire on ANY route)
1. `page.on('pageerror', ...)` — any uncaught JS exception.
2. `page.on('console', msg => msg.type() === 'error')` — including hydration warnings: any console message whose text matches `/Warning: Text content did not match/i` OR `/Hydration failed/i` OR `/Did not expect server HTML/i`.
3. `page.on('requestfailed', ...)` — any failed request (network errors, blocked, aborted).
4. Any response with status `>= 400` (404, 500, etc.) is a failure, EXCEPT when the route's row in the table above has an expected redirect (none of the listed routes do — `/en/login` returns 200 directly).
5. For rows with `expects_shell: true`: missing landmark/region — any of `[data-testid="app-shell"]`, `[data-testid="app-sidebar"]`, `[data-testid="app-topbar"]`, or no `<main>` element, or no `<nav aria-label="Primary">`.
6. For rows with `expects_shell: false`: presence of any of `[data-testid="app-shell"]`, `[data-testid="app-sidebar"]`, or `[data-testid="app-topbar"]` in the response body.
7. Wrong active nav: for rows with `active_nav_item`, that sidebar item must carry `aria-current="page"` AND no other sidebar item may. Same rule for subnav items where applicable.
8. Wrong localized redirect: visiting `/login` (no locale prefix) MUST redirect to `/${default_locale}/login`; the spec asserts the chain and fails on a missing redirect, a 4xx/5xx during the chain, or a redirect to the wrong locale.

Each failure is recorded in `parity_report.json` AND fails the spec.

## Report contract
Write `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/parity_report.json`:
```json
{
  "wave": "app-shell-foundation-20260520",
  "task": "T-136",
  "generated_at": "<ISO-8601>",
  "playwright_version": "<from package.json>",
  "entries": [
    {
      "route": "/en/settings/users",
      "auth": "authed",
      "expects_shell": true,
      "expects_subnav": "settings",
      "active_nav_item": {"sidebar": "settings", "subnav": "users"},
      "screenshot_path": "apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/en-settings-users-desktop-1440.png",
      "console_errors": [{"text": "...", "location": "..."}],
      "hydration_warnings": ["..."],
      "page_errors": ["..."],
      "failed_requests": [{"url": "...", "failure": "..."}],
      "bad_status_responses": [{"url": "...", "status": 500}],
      "missing_regions": ["app-sidebar"],
      "wrong_active_nav": null,
      "wrong_localized_redirect": null,
      "result": "FAIL"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {"pass": 0, "fail": 7, "warn": 0},
  "recommended_followups": [
    {
      "kind": "task_proposal",
      "display_id_hint": "UI-???",
      "category": "ui",
      "reason": "app-sidebar missing on /en/settings/users — UI-131 mount likely broken",
      "evidence_routes": ["/en/settings/users"],
      "prototype_refs": ["prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:3-21"],
      "note": "NOT auto-created. Operator/Opus reviews and decides whether to file via TaskCreate."
    }
  ]
}
```
The schema in `parity_report.schema.json` is the canonical shape.

Follow-up rules:
- Each unique failure category per route produces one `recommended_followups[]` entry.
- The spec MUST NOT call any ACP API, MUST NOT shell out to `kira_dev`, MUST NOT write outside `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/`.
- The spec MUST set `"note": "NOT auto-created. ..."` on every entry.

## Acceptance criteria
1. `apps/web/e2e/shell-smoke.spec.ts` exists, runs under `pnpm exec playwright test e2e/shell-smoke.spec.ts`.
2. The spec exits non-zero if any failure condition (1–8 above) fires on any visited route.
3. The spec writes `parity_report.json` AND a per-route full-page screenshot under `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/`.
4. The report validates against `parity_report.schema.json` (assert with `ajv` inside the spec teardown or a separate vitest unit, your choice).
5. The report contains `recommended_followups[]` populated only on failures; on a fully-passing run the array is empty but present.
6. No ACP task is created by this spec. No file outside `apps/web/e2e/` is modified by the run.
7. `apps/web/proxy.ts` and AppShell components are byte-identical before/after.
8. Locally and in CI the spec is wired into `pnpm exec playwright test` (i.e. picked up by the default test glob).

## Out of scope
- Auto-creating ACP tasks from the report (deliberately a human / Opus decision step).
- Visual regression tooling beyond a screenshot dump.
- Cross-browser matrix (use the project default Playwright browser only).
- Mobile viewports (deferred wave).
- Modifying `proxy.ts`, AppShell product code, or any other task JSON.

## RED test
The spec is itself the RED gate. Run it once before promoting UI-131/UI-132/T-134 (every route fails); run it after they all land (every route should pass).

## Risk red lines
- Do NOT mark `error_discovery_mode: true` here — the wave OVERRIDES the brief's original WARN-only stance; this gate MUST fail closed.
- Do NOT call ACP APIs or create tasks from this spec.
- Do NOT write outside `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/`.
- Do NOT modify the AppShell components when fixing failures uncovered by this spec — file follow-up tasks instead.
- Do NOT modify `apps/web/proxy.ts`.

## Closeout (fail-closed)
Provide: test_commands_and_results (Playwright run output), parity_report.json (committed under `apps/web/e2e/parity-evidence/shell/`), per-route screenshots (also committed), acceptance_criteria_status, changed_files, git_status, deviations_from_prototype, console_network_check (the report IS the check), screenshots, screenshot_or_dom_diff_artifacts, visual_parity_evidence, prototype_reference. `ui_parity_evidence_missing` blocks closeout.

prd_refs: brief Spark-style audit summary: fail on pageerror/console.error/requestfailed/404/500
pipeline_inputs.description: Playwright spec visiting every shell-relevant route in a real browser. Fails closed on pageerror, console.error, requestfailed, 4xx/5xx, hydration errors, missing AppShell regions, wrong active nav item, and wrong localized redirect. Writes machine-readable parity_report.json with recommended_followups[]; does NOT create ACP tasks.
pipeline_inputs.details: spec uses T-123 Playwright harness + auth-storage fixture. Visits /en/, /en/settings, /en/settings/users, /en/settings/roles, /en/login, /en/dev/scanner, /pl/login. Installs page.on('pageerror'/'console'/'requestfailed') spies plus response status monitor. Asserts region presence/absence, aria-current correctness, localized redirect chain. Writes parity_report.json conforming to parity_report.schema.json. Each failure adds a recommended_followups[] entry with note='NOT auto-created…'. No ACP API calls.
